title: KiCraftProjectPlan
description: Topology-level plan for a greenfield KiCad project. Captures functional requirements and circuit topology only -- no exact part numbers, no schematic, no PCB. Produced by the kicraft-opencode-plugin start-new-project flow. Consumed by later KiCraft layers (formalize-design, select-parts).

schema_version.description: Bumped on breaking changes to this schema.

kicraft_layer.description: Identifies which KiCraft layer produced this artifact.

summary.description: One-paragraph human description of the project.

raw_text.description: Verbatim original user request, before any LLM rewriting.

clarifications.description: Q/A pairs collected during the clarifying-questions phase.

requirements.description: Formalized functional and electrical requirements derived from the user spec.

functional.description: What the device must do, in plain language.

electrical.description: Voltage/current/frequency/impedance constraints. Plain text, e.g. 'Input: 5V USB, max 3A'.

mechanical.description: Form factor, mounting, connector orientation, etc.

environmental.description: Temperature range, IP rating, EMI, etc.

interfaces.description: External-facing interfaces: USB-C, JST-PH, RJ45, etc.

must_use_parts.description: Parts the user explicitly named (e.g. 'STM32F411', 'WS2812B'). These are pinned by name only -- exact part-number selection happens in a later layer.

explicitly_excluded.description: Things the user said they do NOT want (e.g. 'no wireless', 'no battery').

topology.description: Circuit topology at the block-diagram level. NO exact part numbers, NO schematic.

blocks.description: Functional blocks of the design, e.g. 'USB-C input + protection', 'Buck converter 5V->3.3V', 'MCU', 'LED driver'.

rationale.description: Why this block exists in the topology.

candidate_part_classes.description: Generic part classes only -- e.g. 'TVS diode', 'synchronous buck IC', 'low-power MCU'. NEVER specific part numbers.

signal_flow.description: Directed edges between blocks describing power and signal flow.

power_tree.description: Sequence of voltage rails and the blocks that produce/consume them.

open_questions.description: Things the LLM still wasn't sure about and recommends resolving before the next layer.

research_notes.description: Useful background the LLM gathered (datasheet refs, reference designs by NAME only). No copyrighted dumps.

source.description: URL or short citation if applicable.

design_priorities.description: Ordered priorities (cost, size, efficiency, BOM-count, etc.).
